purpose: Research-backed frontier capability map for turning every Kolm product surface into a best-in-class implementation program with verifiable smoke checks.

sources.name: OpenAI supervised fine-tuning and distillation flow
Anthropic MCP connector for Messages API
Together inference and GPU cluster product surface
TensorRT-LLM paged attention and in-flight batching
Sigstore Cosign blob signing and verification

competitors.name: Fireworks AI
strengths: Serverless base-model inference with managed billing and lifecycle
Dedicated LoRA deployment modes including live merge and multi-LoRA
Clear performance/cost path for teams that do not want to operate GPUs
kolm_response: Keep OpenAI-compatible hosted and BYOC inference, but make the .kolm artifact portable instead of deployment-bound
Route LoRA and compiled artifacts through the same verifier, receipt, and K-score gates
Expose no-GPU training and distill flows through the cloud compute broker

competitors.name: Together AI
strengths: Broad model catalog, OpenAI-compatible inference, fine-tuning, and GPU clusters
LoRA, full fine-tune, preference fine-tune, function-calling fine-tune, reasoning fine-tune, VLM fine-tune, and BYOM
A unified cloud destination for teams that need hosted training and inference
kolm_response: Provider parity must include OpenAI, Anthropic, Together, Bedrock, local, and self-hosted OpenAI-compatible targets
Kolm must make the output artifact auditable, signed, portable, and runnable after training
Training UX must plan local vs managed vs BYOC compute explicitly

competitors.name: Predibase
strengths: Adapter-first training and multi-LoRA base deployments
LoRAX pattern for many adapters on one GPU
Enterprise story around fine-tuned SLM serving
kolm_response: Kolm must treat multi-adapter serving engines as targets, not as the whole product
Every adapter or distilled artifact needs lineage, diff, verifier, and runtime receipt
Registry network effects should make artifact discovery and governance stronger than a fine-tune dashboard

competitors.name: OpenPipe
strengths: Simple dataset-to-fine-tune flow
Friendly UI for training open and closed model variants
Practical low-friction fine-tuning entry point
kolm_response: Kolm quickstarts must be as easy, but with capture, eval, artifact, and runtime in one loop
The CLI, TUI, and account UI must expose the same guided path
K-score diagnostics must tell users why a compile fails and what to label next

competitors.name: Amazon Bedrock
strengths: Managed distillation and fine-tuning inside AWS procurement and governance
Teacher-student workflow with synthetic data and validation split
Supported model and region matrix
kolm_response: AWS should be a Kolm compute and storage target, not a competitor-only path
Kolm must preserve training/validation split proof and teacher provenance across providers
Enterprise docs must explain when to use Bedrock-native jobs versus portable .kolm outputs

competitors.name: vLLM
strengths: OpenAI-compatible serving surface across chat, responses, embeddings, audio, rerank, and scoring
LoRA support and deployment patterns
Production ecosystem around high-throughput serving
kolm_response: Kolm runtime planner must generate vLLM manifests and smoke checks
Artifacts need OpenAI-compatible conformance probes before being marked deployable
Serving-engine choice should be automatic from workload, model, LoRA count, and hardware

competitors.name: SGLang
strengths: OpenAI-compatible, Ollama-compatible, native, and offline APIs
Multi-LoRA with dynamic loading, eviction, tensor parallelism, and overlap loading
Deep focus on structured generation, deterministic inference, tracing, and long-context serving
kolm_response: Kolm should compile serving plans that can target SGLang for structured and adapter-heavy workloads
LoRA cache pressure, eviction, rank, and target-module limits must appear in runtime diagnostics
Receipts must capture serving backend, adapter id, rank, and cache policy

competitors.name: NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM
strengths: NVIDIA-optimized FP4, FP8, AWQ, GPTQ, and KV cache quantization recipes
Paged attention and in-flight batching for GPU throughput
Strong hardware-specific acceleration story
kolm_response: Quantization oracle must emit TensorRT-LLM-ready recommendations for NVIDIA GPU targets
Runtime profiles need p50/p95/p99 latency, batch policy, and quantization provenance
Compiler lowering must never market generic portability as NVIDIA-optimized without a backend proof

competitors.name: Hugging Face TGI
strengths: Production LLM serving toolkit with streaming, continuous batching, quantization, PagedAttention, OpenTelemetry, and Prometheus
Broad model support and familiar Hugging Face deployment path
Useful baseline for serving and observability expectations
kolm_response: Kolm should test generated artifacts against TGI-style telemetry and OpenAI-compatible contracts
The product must be honest that TGI is a target/baseline, not a replacement category
Observability exports should match the production norms TGI established

competitors.name: ONNX Runtime
strengths: Execution provider matrix across CPU, CUDA, TensorRT, OpenVINO, CoreML, DirectML, QNN, and other targets
Stable runtime API across heterogeneous hardware
Enterprise-friendly deployment familiarity
kolm_response: Artifact runtime metadata must identify EP support, fallback, and unsupported operators
Compiler must surface why an ONNX target is or is not valid for a model family
Device-fit autopilot should pick ONNX Runtime when broad hardware wins over custom engine speed

competitors.name: IREE
strengths: MLIR-derived compilation path across CPU, Vulkan, CUDA, ROCm, Metal, and experimental WebGPU
Clear separation between compiler targets and runtime HAL drivers
A real compiler architecture for heterogeneous deployment
kolm_response: Kolm IR needs a target-lowering matrix instead of vague compiler language
Every runtime target must define input dialect, lowering path, runtime driver, and conformance probe
The .kolm spec should distinguish artifact contract from engine-specific executable payloads

competitors.name: ExecuTorch
strengths: PyTorch-native on-device inference for mobile, embedded, wearables, NPUs, and DSPs
Edge deployment productivity from export to runtime
Relevant for consumer/mobile/offline-first AI apps
kolm_response: Mobile SDKs and runtime packages must be real release artifacts, not docs-only claims
Device-fit autopilot must include battery, memory, NPU, and offline-mode checks
Artifact receipts should include device target and runtime version

competitors.name: LangSmith, Braintrust, Phoenix, and Weave
strengths: Production tracing, datasets, evals, prompt iteration, experiments, alerts, and monitors
Strong operator UX for debugging non-deterministic AI systems
OpenTelemetry and provider integrations are becoming expected
kolm_response: Kolm must export OTEL GenAI spans, receipt references, eval rows, and dataset lineage instead of trapping logs
K-score needs trace-to-eval replay and external eval export
Account UI and CLI should make failed cases labelable immediately

competitors.name: Cloudflare R2 and Workers AI
strengths: S3-compatible R2 object storage and serverless AI/Workers platform
Global network, AI Gateway, Vectorize, R2, Durable Objects, D1, and Workers all in one developer surface
Practical deploy target for edge inference and artifact distribution
kolm_response: Cloudflare R2 should be a first-class storage backend for artifacts, lake exports, and benchmark fixtures
Workers/Edge deployments should have generated manifests and probe commands
Temporary credentials and claim-scoped object policies need to be part of enterprise controls

competitors.name: Sigstore, SLSA, and CycloneDX
strengths: Industry-recognized signing, verification bundle, transparency log, and artifact proof patterns
A clear trust model beyond proprietary signatures
Procurement-friendly evidence path
kolm_response: .kolm signatures should keep local Ed25519 verification and add optional Sigstore bundles for public artifacts
Compliance exports need SBOM/SLSA/CycloneDX-compatible packets
Verifier CLI should work offline and disclose which trust roots were used

programs.product_gap: Provider choice currently exists as a capability, but best-in-class users expect OpenAI, Anthropic, hosted open model clouds, Bedrock, local, and self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints to be interchangeable across capture, teacher generation, training, and runtime.
programs.invention: Provider Parity Router: one policy engine that scores provider/model choices by quality gate, latency, cost ceiling, data residency, tool support, and allowed teacher licenses before every capture, distill, compile, or runtime call.
build_steps: Normalize provider descriptors for OpenAI, Anthropic, Together, Bedrock, Cloudflare Workers AI, local .kolm, and generic OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
Attach model capabilities: chat, responses, tools, vision, embeddings, fine-tune, distill, streaming, JSON mode, MCP, and data-residency constraints.
Add a solver that rejects providers violating privacy, budget, latency, or modality constraints before ranking candidates.
Persist the selected provider and fallback chain into compile receipts and event-lake rows.
Expose the same provider override UX in CLI, TUI, account UI, and OpenAPI.
Add a provider dry-run mode that returns reasons, rejected candidates, and required env vars without sending data.
acceptance_tests: OpenAI and Anthropic teacher paths both produce compatible distill job envelopes without changing downstream compile code.
A no-GPU user with Cloudflare R2 and RunPod/Together/AWS env vars gets a valid remote training plan instead of a local hardware blocker.
A HIPAA zero-retention policy rejects providers without allowed retention and emits an actionable diagnostic.
Fallback chain receipts record primary, backup, policy reason, and whether user data left the configured boundary.

programs.product_gap: Kolm has distill and training surfaces, but the user experience must choose between SFT, DPO/preference, RFT-style graders, synthetic teacher labeling, LoRA, full fine-tune, and managed distillation without making the user understand vendor quirks first.
programs.invention: Teacher Training Orchestrator: a strategy compiler that converts examples, evals, model constraints, and budget into a staged training plan with teacher calls, train/holdout separation, labels to collect next, provider job adapters, and rollback thresholds.
build_steps: Add task classifier for extraction, classification, generation, tool calling, vision-language, retrieval, and agent actions.
Map task class to viable training methods: prompt-only, distillation, LoRA, full fine-tune, preference fine-tune, RFT-style grader loop, or no-train recommendation.
Attach provider-specific job adapters for OpenAI, Together, Bedrock, Predibase-compatible LoRA exports, local Axolotl/TRL, and future BYOC runners.
Preserve train/holdout split hashes and teacher provenance through every exported job.
Estimate cost, wall time, storage, and likely quality gain before submitting work.
Add webhook and polling envelopes that converge into the same compile receipt.
acceptance_tests: A 20-row dataset gets a no-train or collect-more-data diagnostic instead of an overconfident fine-tune plan.
A 2,000-row extraction task with strict schema selects LoRA or distillation and preserves holdout hashes.
A reasoning-heavy legal task recommends grader-backed evaluation before RFT-style or large-teacher distillation.
Provider job adapters produce normalized status, artifact, metric, and failure diagnostics.

programs.product_gap: A finished AI compiler needs serving-engine literacy. vLLM, SGLang, TGI, TensorRT-LLM, llama.cpp, and ONNX Runtime have different strengths; Kolm should plan and verify a target instead of presenting runtime as a generic checkbox.
programs.invention: Serving Engine Fabric: an engine planner that benchmarks or simulates workload shape, LoRA count, context length, streaming needs, hardware, and observability requirements, then emits a runnable deployment manifest and conformance probe.
build_steps: Create engine descriptors for vLLM, SGLang, TGI, TensorRT-LLM, ONNX Runtime, llama.cpp/GGUF, MLX, CoreML, and ExecuTorch.
Define capability probes for OpenAI-compatible chat, responses, embeddings, streaming, tool calls, structured outputs, LoRA, multi-modal, and telemetry.
Add workload scoring for context length, batch shape, adapter count, cold-start tolerance, target latency, and hardware.
Generate Docker, SSH, BYOC, and local launch manifests with health checks and rollback commands.
Write receipts with engine version, quantization, adapter policy, model hash, and conformance result.
Expose a dry-run diff comparing two engine plans.
acceptance_tests: An adapter-heavy workload selects SGLang, LoRAX-compatible, or vLLM LoRA over a generic TGI plan with reason codes.
A low-latency NVIDIA plan recommends TensorRT-LLM only when model and hardware support match.
A broad enterprise hardware plan recommends ONNX Runtime/OpenVINO/QNN when portability outranks absolute throughput.
Every generated plan has a health endpoint, OpenAI-compatible probe, receipt fields, and telemetry export config.

programs.product_gap: The term compiler is only defensible if each output target has explicit input dialect, lowering, verifier, runtime, and fallback semantics.
programs.invention: Kolm IR Lowering Matrix: a contract table that maps .kolm recipe features to GGUF, ONNX, CoreML, MLX, ExecuTorch, LiteRT, WASM/WebGPU, TensorRT-LLM, vLLM, SGLang, and TGI, including unsupported-feature diagnostics.
build_steps: Define a normalized recipe IR for prompts, tools, schemas, eval gates, model payloads, tokenizers, adapters, and receipt requirements.
Enumerate target runtimes and supported payload families with versioned compatibility ranges.
Add lowering diagnostics for unsupported modality, tokenizer, quantization, operator, schema, or streaming semantics.
Emit target manifests with verifier expectations and runtime probe commands.
Add conformance fixtures for one extraction, one classifier, one tool-call, one RAG, one multimodal, and one agent artifact.
Publish the matrix into docs and product graph so UI surfaces cannot overclaim unsupported targets.
acceptance_tests: Every artifact-runtime option in product-graph has a target descriptor and at least one conformance fixture.
Unsupported targets return structured reasons instead of silent fallback.
WASM/WebGPU package-release readiness stays marked external until package artifacts are actually publishable.
Third-party runtime adoption remains external until external support is linked.

programs.product_gap: A generic quantize button is not enough. Users need a target-aware recommendation across AWQ, GPTQ, FP8, FP4, KV-cache quantization, ONNX EPs, mobile runtimes, and quality-risk gates.
programs.invention: Quantization Runtime Oracle: an advisor that chooses the smallest safe representation for the task, model family, target device, context length, and K-score budget, then records rejected quantization options and why.
build_steps: Extend model descriptors with supported quantization recipes by model family and runtime.
Add calibration-set scoring for task-specific degradation rather than relying on perplexity only.
Add hardware compatibility matrix for NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, Apple, Qualcomm, CPU, browser, and edge.
Emit quantization manifests with method, calibration rows, held-out score delta, and rollback target.
Add public benchmark export for every quantization claim before marketing it.
Tie runtime telemetry back into future quantization recommendations.
acceptance_tests: A 7B extraction workload on RTX selects an aggressive recipe only when K-score loss stays under threshold.
A mobile Qualcomm target rejects unsupported FP8 paths and suggests QNN/ExecuTorch-compatible outputs.
A long-context workload accounts for KV cache memory and quality degradation.
Benchmarking-infra remains in needs_public_benchmark_data until external reproducible data exists.

programs.product_gap: Kolm's proof story is strongest when K-score, receipts, traces, datasets, and replay are one auditable workflow. Competing observability tools set the UX expectation for trace search, eval runs, experiment comparison, and monitors.
programs.invention: Eval Observability Spine: OpenTelemetry GenAI export plus trace-to-dataset replay, K-score drilldown, production shadow evals, and external eval exports for Braintrust/Phoenix/LangSmith/Weave-compatible workflows.
build_steps: Emit OTEL GenAI spans for provider calls, retrieval, tool calls, distill jobs, evals, and runtime inference.
Store sensitive content by policy: inline, redacted, external reference, or zero-retention hash only.
Create trace-to-dataset promotion with human labels, auto-label suggestions, and provenance hashes.
Add replay against prompt, model, artifact, and runtime variants with statistical comparison.
Export datasets and eval results in formats accepted by external eval tooling.
Make account and CLI failures immediately actionable with label-next, replay, and compile-next affordances.
acceptance_tests: A captured production failure can be promoted to a dataset row and replayed against an artifact candidate.
OTEL spans include required GenAI provider/model/token/error fields and redact content under zero-retention policy.
K-score detail links to row-level eval evidence and train/holdout provenance.
External export smoke creates Braintrust/Phoenix-style dataset rows without leaking secrets.

programs.product_gap: Enterprise buyers need selectable retention, redaction, tenant isolation, CMK/BYOK, storage residency, audit logs, and formal certification evidence. Local tests cannot honestly replace certification, but the product can make the evidence factory complete.
programs.invention: Privacy Governance Membrane: policy compiler for zero retention, redacted capture, differential privacy, encrypted lake exports, tenant RBAC, storage residency, and audit-evidence packets.
build_steps: Define privacy modes as executable policy, not page copy: zero-retention, redacted-retention, local-only, BYOC, air-gap, and federated.
Add CMK/BYOK hooks and object-storage encryption metadata to lake and artifact exports.
Ensure every capture path either stores redacted content or hash-only proof depending on policy.
Generate compliance evidence packets with control owner, evidence file, date, and auditor-ready notes.
Keep formal SOC 2, ISO, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP, SLSA, and SBOM rows marked external until live proof exists.
Add tenant-isolation tests for all new policy paths.
acceptance_tests: Zero-retention requests do not persist raw prompt/output content while preserving hash and receipt proof.
BYOC/R2/S3 exports include storage region, encryption mode, and key reference without exposing key material.
Compliance-certifications remains a live-certification gate with generated evidence packet only.
Team RBAC prevents a user from reading another tenant's lake rows, artifacts, or receipts.

programs.product_gap: Users without local GPUs must still be able to train, distill, compile, benchmark, and deploy. Enterprise users need BYOC and storage controls instead of a black-box Kolm cloud only.
programs.invention: Cloud Compute Control Plane: a broker that chooses local, SSH, managed GPU, AWS, Cloudflare, RunPod, Together, Modal, or BYOC workers, binds them to R2/S3/Supabase/local storage, and returns deterministic job receipts.
build_steps: Normalize compute provider descriptors with required env vars, GPU SKUs, data plane, pricing model, and privacy notes.
Normalize storage backends for local, R2-compatible, S3-compatible, AWS S3/KMS, Supabase Storage, and air-gap bundles.
Add a dry-run planner that chooses compute and storage from workload, model size, rows, privacy, cost, and deadline.
Implement job submission adapters behind a queue abstraction with idempotency keys.
Attach webhook, polling, cancellation, retry, and artifact fetch contracts.
Add production-readiness diagnostics for missing env vars and insufficient quotas without failing unrelated local flows.
acceptance_tests: No-local-GPU training returns a remote compute plan with storage staging and explicit costs.
A BYOC enterprise policy rejects public managed workers and chooses SSH/AWS VPC targets.
R2-compatible storage config passes S3 endpoint, region, key, and object prefix validation.
Job receipts include compute provider, storage backend, artifact hash, and retry lineage.

programs.product_gap: Local SDK code is not the same thing as production distribution. Public npm, PyPI, crates, Homebrew, winget, apt, Swift, Kotlin, React Native, WASM, and MCP package releases need structural gates.
programs.invention: Package Release Factory: a manifest-driven package publisher that builds, smoke-installs, signs, and documents every distribution channel while keeping unreleased channels honest in readiness data.
build_steps: Define package manifests for npm, PyPI, crates, Homebrew, winget, apt, SwiftPM, Kotlin/Maven, React Native, WASM, MCP, and VS Code.
Run local build and smoke-install commands for every package that can be tested without external credentials.
Add signing and provenance hooks for public artifacts.
Generate release readiness JSON consumed by docs, CLI, account UI, and release verifier.
Keep external package publication gates marked needs_package_release until package registries confirm publication.
Add doctor diagnostics that tell users the exact supported install path per platform.
acceptance_tests: Every SDK/package row has a source path, build command, smoke command, and publication status.
Unpublished package channels cannot be advertised as one-line installs.
WASM, iOS, Android, and React Native rows stay external until package artifacts are built and publishable.
Package manifests and docs use the same package names.

expected: sdk-depth, one-line-install, runtime-wasm, ios-android-sdk gates addressed

programs.product_gap: To become a format winner, Kolm needs artifact discovery, trust, versioning, verified publishers, private registries, dependency graphs, and deploy buttons that feel closer to Docker Hub plus npm than a static recipe gallery.
programs.invention: Registry Network Effects: a verified artifact registry that makes .kolm files searchable, diffable, signed, deployable, privately mirrorable, and dependency-aware.
build_steps: Index artifact metadata by task, model family, runtime, compliance tag, license, K-score, publisher, and dependency graph.
Add version diff views for prompt, schema, model, quantization, eval, receipt, and runtime changes.
Implement verified publisher badges backed by signing keys and organization verification.
Generate deploy buttons for local, Docker, SSH, AWS, Cloudflare, Vercel, and BYOC targets.
Support private registry mirrors with export/import and air-gap bundle paths.
Add marketplace transaction hooks later, but keep current scope focused on trust and deployability.
acceptance_tests: A public artifact listing exposes hash, signature, K-score, runtime targets, license, and dependency graph.
A private registry export can be verified offline.
Version diff shows material behavior and proof changes, not only text metadata.
Deploy buttons are generated only for runtime targets with passing conformance probes.

programs.product_gap: On-device AI requires more than saying local. Phones, laptops, browsers, edge gateways, NPUs, and air-gapped servers have different memory, power, runtime, and packaging constraints.
programs.invention: Device Runtime Autopilot: a target planner that profiles device class and chooses CoreML/MLX, ONNX Runtime EP, ExecuTorch, LiteRT, WASM/WebGPU, OpenVINO, QNN, GGUF, or cloud fallback.
build_steps: Define device profiles for iOS, Android, Apple Silicon, Windows NPU, Intel OpenVINO, Qualcomm QNN, browser WebGPU, edge Linux, and CPU-only.
Estimate memory, battery, p95 latency, cold start, context budget, and offline constraints.
Select runtime target and quantization jointly rather than independently.
Generate SDK integration snippets and offline verifier commands per target.
Add package-release gates for mobile/WASM artifacts and keep external status honest.
Record device profile and runtime version in each inference receipt.
acceptance_tests: An Android/QNN target rejects CoreML-only outputs and offers ExecuTorch/ONNX/QNN options.
A browser target uses WASM/WebGPU readiness and does not advertise unpublished package release as done.
A CPU-only air-gap target produces a GGUF/ONNX fallback with expected latency warning.
Device receipts include runtime version, quantization, model hash, and offline mode.

programs.product_gap: Agent tools are becoming distributed software artifacts. Kolm should be the signed, testable, auditable way to ship agent behavior and MCP tools, not just an API wrapper.
programs.invention: Agent Tool Supply Chain: compile .kolm artifacts into MCP servers and tool bundles with schema tests, policy limits, signed dependencies, runtime receipts, and Claude/OpenAI-compatible invocation probes.
build_steps: Generate MCP tool descriptors from compiled artifacts and schemas.
Add tool input/output conformance tests and prompt-injection budget checks.
Support Claude Desktop/Claude Code, Anthropic Messages MCP connector, OpenAI tool calling, and local MCP hosts.
Sign tool bundles and dependency manifests.
Emit OTEL spans and receipts for every tool call.
Publish registry metadata that lets enterprises approve, pin, or revoke agent tools.
acceptance_tests: kolm compile --as-mcp produces a local MCP server with tool schema and verifier command.
Tool calls enforce token budget, data policy, and allowed provider constraints.
Signed dependency graph lets a registry revoke or pin a tool artifact.
Anthropic and OpenAI tool-call probe fixtures both pass against the generated server contract.

programs.product_gap: The $10B path depends on .kolm becoming an ecosystem artifact format. That requires public spec governance, verifier portability, conformance tests, compatibility policy, and external adoption proof.
programs.invention: Format Governance Standard: a standards packet that turns .kolm v1 into an RFC-grade artifact spec with conformance suite, verifier CLI, compatibility badges, Sigstore optional bundle, and external adoption ledger.
build_steps: Publish RFC-style spec sections for ZIP layout, manifest schema, runtime payloads, signatures, receipts, eval gates, and compatibility.
Define conformance fixtures and expected verifier output for valid, tampered, expired, unsupported, and future-version artifacts.
Add optional Sigstore bundle support while keeping offline Ed25519 verification.
Generate external submission packet for foundation or standards venue.
Track external adoption evidence separately from local implementation evidence.
Fail docs/release checks if external adoption is claimed without linked proof.
acceptance_tests: A third-party verifier can validate a fixture without hosted Kolm.
Spec examples match machine-readable schemas and conformance outputs.
Foundation-standardization remains external until accepted venue evidence exists.
Ecosystem-runtime-adoption remains external until external projects publish support.

programs.product_gap: Benchmark claims are the credibility engine. Local tests prove contracts; they do not prove public performance leadership. Kolm needs reproducible public benchmark packets with hardware, model, dataset, runtime, cost, and quality metadata.
programs.invention: Benchmark Evidence Network: a reproducible benchmark harness that publishes signed result packets for provider, distill, quantize, compile, and runtime comparisons, while preventing stale or unverified claims from reaching public copy.
build_steps: Define benchmark packet schema with task, dataset hash, model, runtime, hardware, quantization, provider, cost, latency, quality, and date.
Add local reproducibility harness and remote runner hooks.
Sign benchmark packets and link them to artifact versions.
Add stale-claim scanner that rejects public claims lacking current benchmark packets.
Publish minimal public fixtures first, then expand to hosted leaderboard.
Keep benchmarking-infra status as needs_public_benchmark_data until public packets are posted.
acceptance_tests: A benchmark result cannot be public-ready without signed hardware, dataset, and model metadata.
Stale public claims fail release verification when benchmark packet date or hash is missing.
Kolm vs provider comparisons include quality, latency, cost, and artifact size.
Public benchmark gate remains external until reproducible packet URLs are available.

programs.product_gap: The capture lake is the product flywheel, but it only creates durable value if users can query, filter, redact, label, export, and recompile from it without breaking privacy boundaries.
programs.invention: Data Lake Governance Loop: capture-to-label-to-eval-to-compile workflow with SQL/Parquet export, OTEL spans, privacy policies, active-learning ranking, and storage adapters.
build_steps: Expose lake schema and query/export commands for JSONL, Parquet-compatible metadata, and SQL-like filters.
Add capture filters by provider, model, latency, cost, error, endpoint, user, team, and policy.
Rank unlabeled examples by expected K-score improvement and distribution coverage.
Promote selected rows into datasets while preserving tenant, redaction, consent, and holdout metadata.
Allow one-click or CLI recompile from a filtered dataset snapshot.
Export lake evidence to external eval tools without raw secret leakage.
acceptance_tests: A user can filter high-cost GPT calls, redact them, label a subset, and create a compile dataset.
Holdout-only and tenant metadata survive export/import.
Differential privacy policy changes the exported aggregates and records epsilon/delta metadata.
Zero-retention lake mode supports hash-only analytics without raw prompt storage.

programs.product_gap: Text-only artifacts are not enough for frontier product depth. Users need image, audio, embedding, rerank, classifier, tool-call, and structured-output artifact targets where feasible, with honest unsupported diagnostics where not.
programs.invention: Multimodal Tokenization Targets: a target contract for tokenizer, processor, modality, schema, and runtime support that prevents text-only assumptions from leaking into compile, runtime, SDK, and docs.
build_steps: Classify model families by text, vision-language, audio, embedding, rerank, classifier, and tool-call support.
Represent tokenizer and processor hashes in artifact manifests.
Add runtime-specific modality support checks for vLLM, SGLang, TGI, ONNX Runtime, ExecuTorch, CoreML, and WASM.
Extend compile diagnostics to explain unsupported processors or missing model assets.
Add fixtures for multimodal and embedding artifacts where local-only tests can be deterministic.
Make docs and product graph consume the same capability matrix.
acceptance_tests: A vision-language compile plan records image processor hash and runtime support.
An embedding/rerank artifact does not route through chat-only runtime assumptions.
Unsupported multimodal targets return structured diagnostics with alternate targets.
Mobile and WASM package gates remain honest until package artifacts exist.
